svg.text: SOAR-Light — Kafka Topics & gRPC-In Complete Integration Map
C2C Kafka Topics | On-Prem gRPC Topics | kafka.flow.processor Routing | grpc-in as On-Prem Broker
C2C (Cloud) — Kafka Topic Architecture & Message Routing
Created by cdc.api when flow is configured
Filters by ecosystem → Routes to destinations
Complete Kafka Topic Naming for SOAR-Light (C2C):
Layer 1 — Raw Topics (published by cdc.api / BloxOne ingestion):
acme-corp_flowID42_TD_QUERY_RESP_LOG ← All TD DNS query logs for flow 42
acme-corp_flowID42_DDI_DHCP_LEASE_LOG ← All DDI DHCP lease logs for flow 42
acme-corp_flowID42_AUDIT_LOG ← All audit logs for flow 42
acme-corp_flowID42_SOC_INSIGHTS ← SOC insight events for flow 42
Layer 2 — Destination Topics (published by kafka.flow.processor routing):
acme-corp_42_SOAR_LIGHT_TD_QUERY_RESP_LOG ← SOAR-Light consumer: TD DNS queries
acme-corp_42_SOAR_LIGHT_SOC_INSIGHTS_V2 ← SOAR-Light consumer: SOC insights v2
← Shared across all data types of flow 42 (each data type = separate consumer in same group)
On-Prem — gRPC Internal Topic Architecture (grpc-in as Message Broker)
1. Receives Parquet/CSV from cloud (Dapr) or NIOS (file)
2. Parses data into events, caches by topic
3. Serves events via gRPC stream on port 9195
Acts as Kafka replacement for on-prem deployments
← Note: NO account_id prefix (on-prem has single account)
Each Python process subscribes to exactly ONE topic. 3 data types = 3 Python processes connecting to grpc-in:9195.
Side-by-Side: Cloud (C2C) vs On-Prem Topic & Transport Comparison
Complete End-to-End: grpc-in's Role in SOAR-Light On-Prem Data Pipeline
Path A: Cloud Data → On-Prem SOAR-Light
Path B: NIOS Direct → On-Prem SOAR-Light
grpc-in serves as the on-prem equivalent of Kafka — it receives data from multiple upstream sources, indexes by {flowId}_{dataType},
and streams to soar-light via gRPC bidirectional stream. The Python consumer code path differs only in transport (Kafka vs gRPC), not in processing logic.
Both paths end at the same point: plugin.process(context, event) calling the user's IEventHandler implementation → sending alerts/tickets to external SOAR platforms.
